perturbation.original_text: North American and AZZ are sometimes herein collectively referred to as the “ Parties ” and each, a “ Party ”. In its capacity as a Party receiving Confidential Information, as that term is hereinafter defined, a Party may be referred to herein as a “ Receiving Party ”; in its capacity as a Party disclosing Confidential Information, a Party may be referred to herein as a “ Disclosing Party ”.
perturbation.changed_text: North American and AZZ are sometimes herein collectively referred to as the “ Parties ”. In its capacity as a Party receiving Confidential Information a Party may be referred to herein as a “ Receiving Party ”; in its capacity as a Party disclosing information, a Party may be referred to herein as a “ Disclosing Party ”.
perturbation.explanation: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information, as that term is hereinafter defined'. The modified text removes this explicit reference to a subsequent definition. This creates an in-text contradiction because later sections of the agreement rely on a specific definition of 'Confidential Information' (Section 1), but the initial definition in the introduction no longer acknowledges that a formal definition will be provided. The contract becomes internally inconsistent because the initial reference to a defined term is removed, while other clauses still depend on that definition existing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information, Representatives . The Parties are considering entering into a possible transaction (the “ Transaction ”), and in order to assist the other Party in evaluating the possible Transaction, each Party is prepared to make available to the other Party certain confidential, non-public or proprietary information concerning its and/or its affiliates’ and/or subsidiaries’ businesses, plans, operations and assets (any and all such information provided to a Receiving Party by or on behalf a Disclosing Party, its affiliates or subsidiaries regardless of the manner in which such is provided, together with all notes, summaries, analysis, compilations, studies, interpretations and memoranda based thereon or extracts, copies and other reproductions thereof is herein referred to as the “ Confidential Information ”). As a condition to the Confidential Information being furnished by each Party to the other Party and its affiliates, subsidiaries, directors, officers, partners, members, employees, agents, advisors, attorneys, accountants, consultants and potential sources of capital or financing (collectively, “ Representatives ”), each Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information provided to it by the other Party in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement and to take or abstain from taking certain other actions hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that 'Confidential Information' is 'hereinafter defined', but the changed text removes that crucial phrase. Section 1 contains the definition of 'Confidential Information'. The removal of the 'hereinafter defined' clause in the introduction creates a contradiction by suggesting there might not be a specific definition, even though Section 1 provides one. This weakens the contract and introduces ambiguity, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall return all copies of the Confidential Information provided by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party to the Disclosing Party, and all other Confidential Information (including, without limitation, all notes, summaries, analysis, compilations, studies, interpretations and memoranda based thereon or extracts, copies and other reproductions thereof or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect such Confidential Information) shall be destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall return all copies of the Confidential Information provided by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text removes the clause that requires the destruction of 'all other Confidential Information (including, without limitation, all notes, summaries, analysis, compilations, studies, interpretations and memoranda based thereon or extracts, copies and other reproductions thereof or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect such Confidential Information)'. This omission creates an in-text contradiction. Section 5 now states that the Receiving Party must return 'all copies of the Confidential Information', but it no longer explicitly requires the destruction of derivative works containing or reflecting that information. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty, as the Receiving Party could argue that it has complied with Section 5 by returning the original Confidential Information while retaining analyses, summaries, etc., made from it. This contradicts the spirit and original intent of the agreement, which aimed to protect all forms of the Disclosing Party's confidential data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use and Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information . The Receiving Party and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Information provided to it by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction. The Receiving Party shall keep the Confidential Information provided to it by the Disclosing Party confidential and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information provided to it by the Disclosing Party to which the Disclosing Party gives its prior written consent, and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible 1 Transaction and who agree to keep such information confidential. Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 5 required both the return of Confidential Information and the destruction of any derivative works (notes, summaries, etc.) based on that information. The changed text removes the requirement to destroy derivative works. This directly contradicts the implied intent of the 'Use and Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information' clause in another section. The contradicted text intends to protect all Confidential Information from disclosure in any manner, including information 'contained in the Confidential Information.' The omission in Section 5 now allows the Receiving Party to retain and potentially use or disclose analyses and summaries derived from the Confidential Information, which is a clear conflict. The changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction because it undermines the confidentiality protections that the original agreement intended to impose on all forms of the Disclosing Party's confidential data.

perturbation.original_text: Unless and until a definitive written agreement between the Parties with respect to a Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither Party shall be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Transaction by virtue of this or any other written or oral expression by either of them or their Representatives except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless and until a definitive written agreement between the Parties with respect to a Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither Party shall be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Transaction by virtue of this or any other written or oral expression by either of them or their Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein' the edited agreement introduces an internal contradiction. The original clause explicitly states that the confidentiality agreement itself creates legal obligations even in the absence of a final transaction agreement. Removing this exception implies that the confidentiality agreement might not create any binding obligations until a final deal is reached. This contradicts the entire purpose of the confidentiality agreement, which is to protect information during the negotiation phase, *before* a definitive agreement exists.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies . Each Party acknowledges that in the event of any breach of the terms of this Agreement, the other Party could not be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, each Party, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled in law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement. A breaching Party shall reimburse the other Party for all reasonable costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees, incurred by the other Party in the event it successfully enforces the obligations of the breaching Party or its Representatives hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text carves out an exception for the current agreement, explicitly stating that the confidentiality agreement itself creates legal obligations. The changed text removes this exception, creating a contradiction with the 'Remedies' section. The 'Remedies' section clearly states that breaches of 'this Agreement' (the confidentiality agreement) can result in injunctions and specific performance. If the confidentiality agreement doesn't create any legal obligations until a final agreement is reached (as implied by the changed text), then the 'Remedies' section becomes unenforceable, which is a clear and direct contradiction. This follows the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party hereby agrees to fully comply with all applicable securities laws.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party hereby agrees to comply with securities laws.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the word 'fully' creates an in-text contradiction. Section 9 states that each party will comply with all applicable securities laws, but the omission of the word 'fully' leaves room for interpretation regarding the extent of compliance required. This introduces ambiguity and could allow a party to argue that they were not required to strictly adhere to every aspect of the securities laws, which directly contradicts the intent and language of the original statement. It suggests partial compliance might be acceptable, which conflicts with the seriousness implied in the section's first sentences.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Applicable Securities Laws . Each Party hereby acknowledges that some or all of the Confidential Information is likely to constitute material, non-public information. Each Party hereby represents and warrants that it is aware (and that it shall advise its Representatives who are not already aware) that United States and other applicable securities laws prohibit any person who has material, non-public information concerning a publicly traded company or entity from purchasing or selling securities of such company or entity, or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities. Each Party hereby agrees to comply with securities laws.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'Each Party hereby agrees to fully comply with all applicable securities laws.' The changed text removes the word 'fully,' resulting in 'Each Party hereby agrees to comply with securities laws.' According to the explanation, removing 'fully' introduces ambiguity and allows a party to argue that they are not required to strictly adhere to every aspect of the securities laws. This directly contradicts the intent and language of the original statement, and the contradicted text, which emphasizes the importance of each party acknowledging that some or all of the Confidential Information is likely to constitute material, non-public information, and states 'Each Party hereby agrees to comply with securities laws.' The removal of 'fully' from the agreement to comply creates a strong contradiction as the original, stricter, obligation is weakened, potentially conflicting with the intended full adherence implied elsewhere.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, each Party agrees that it shall not, and that it shall cause is affiliates and subsidiaries who have received the other Party’s Confidential Information, or who are aware of the possibility of the Transaction, not to, directly or indirectly, unless specifically invited in advance by the other Party’s Board of Directors: (i) acquire or agree, offer, seek or propose to acquire, or sell or otherwise dispose of, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) of any of the other Party’s assets or businesses or any securities issued by such other Party, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership (including from a third party), (ii) seek or propose to influence or control of the other Party’s management or policies (or request permission to do so), or (iii) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, each Party agrees that it shall not, directly or indirectly, unless specifically invited in advance by the other Party’s Board of Directors: (i) acquire or agree, offer, seek or propose to acquire, or sell or otherwise dispose of, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) of any of the other Party’s assets or businesses or any securities issued by such other Party, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership (including from a third party), (ii) seek or propose to influence or control of the other Party’s management or policies (or request permission to do so), or (iii) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'and that it shall cause is affiliates and subsidiaries who have received the other Party’s Confidential Information, or who are aware of the possibility of the Transaction, not to' is removed, creating a contradiction in Section 10. While the main party is still bound by the standstill agreement, their affiliates and subsidiaries are no longer explicitly obligated, introducing an in-text contradiction. This means the main party can't directly act against the standstill, but their affiliates and subsidiaries, now not bound by the clause, potentially could. This weakens the standstill agreement, as the other party's board has no power to prevent their actions, defeating the purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Standstill . For a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, each Party agrees that it shall not, directly or indirectly, unless specifically invited in advance by the other Party’s Board of Directors: (i) acquire or agree, offer, seek or propose to acquire, or sell or otherwise dispose of, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) of any of the other Party’s assets or businesses or any securities issued by such other Party, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership (including from a third party), (ii) seek or propose to influence or control of the other Party’s management or policies (or request permission to do so), or (iii) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes affiliates and subsidiaries in the standstill agreement. The changed text omits the phrase 'and that it shall cause is affiliates and subsidiaries who have received the other Party’s Confidential Information, or who are aware of the possibility of the Transaction, not to'. This omission creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which imposes a standstill on 'each Party'. While the changed text still binds the primary party, it no longer explicitly binds its affiliates and subsidiaries. This weakens the standstill agreement as the main party can't directly act, but their affiliates can, as the other party's board has no power to prevent their actions. The explanation provided confirms this results in an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Except with regard to paragraph 10, this Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'Except with regard to paragraph 10,' creates an internal contradiction. Originally, paragraph 10 (Standstill) was intended to survive for only one year, while the remainder of the agreement, including confidentiality, lasted for two. Removing the exception suggests the entire agreement now expires after two years, including the standstill provision in paragraph 10. However, Section 10 explicitly states a one-year period for the Standstill. This directly contradicts the term of the standstill agreement itself, creating uncertainty about its duration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Standstill . For a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, each Party agrees that it shall not, and that it shall cause is affiliates and subsidiaries who have received the other Party’s Confidential Information, or who are aware of the possibility of the Transaction, not to, directly or indirectly, unless specifically invited in advance by the other Party’s Board of Directors: (i) acquire or agree, offer, seek or propose to acquire, or sell or otherwise dispose of, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) of any of the other Party’s assets or businesses or any securities issued by such other Party, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership (including from a third party), (ii) seek or propose to influence or control of the other Party’s management or policies (or request permission to do so), or (iii) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The original text creates an exception for paragraph 10, stating the agreement expires in two years except for paragraph 10. The changed text removes the exception, implying the entire agreement, including paragraph 10 (Standstill), expires in two years. However, the contradicted text (paragraph 10) explicitly states the Standstill provision is for one year. This creates a direct conflict, as the changed text implies a two-year duration for the Standstill, contradicting the Standstill's specified one-year duration.
